ShortDescription.Value: Make citizen benefits (pension, unemployment, family allowance) cost the city treasury and scale them, with treasury funding administered by welfare offices.

Publish.LongDescription:
Vanilla Cities: Skylines II "mints" pensions, unemployment and family allowance straight into households at no cost to the city. Realistic Funding & Management: Welfare Benefits turns benefits into a real budget item and gives you control over them.
- Treasury-funded benefits: the real daily outlay is deducted from the city treasury and shown as a budget expense (Subsidies). OFF BY DEFAULT — turn it on to enable; off = exact vanilla behaviour. - Scale each benefit 0-200% (100% = vanilla) — pension, unemployment, family allowance. - Welfare-office gate: with funding on, benefits are charged to the treasury only while your city has a welfare office to administer them. No office = benefits fall back to the base game (still paid, minted free, not charged to the treasury), so your city keeps growing normally, and a warning reminds you to build a welfare office to actually fund them from the treasury.
Recipient counts mirror the game's own rule exactly (living non-workers: children/teens = family allowance, elderly = pension, adults = unemployment while still inside the allowance window).
This mod is one of three that replace the older all-in-one "Economy Tweaks" mod. The other two are "Private Schools & Hospitals" and "Service & Sanitation Levy". Do not run Economy Tweaks alongside these.
Source (open source, MIT): https://github.com/AmicusDeus/WelfareManagement
Made with Claude Code's Opus 4.8 and Fable 5 models.

ChangeLog.Value: v1.22 — Treasury funding no longer breaks immigration when you have no welfare office. Before, turning treasury funding ON without a welfare office zeroed all benefits, which stopped working-age residents and families from moving in (only elderly could) and could stall a new city's growth entirely. Now that state falls back to the base game instead: benefits are still paid (minted free, not charged to the treasury) so the city keeps growing, and a settings warning reminds you to build a welfare office to fund benefits from the treasury. Building the office switches funding on as before.
